categories.label: Triggers
categories.description: Entry points that start a workflow execution

categories.label: Actions
categories.description: Tool calls, HTTP requests, and side-effect operations

categories.label: Logic
categories.description: Branching, merging, and flow control nodes

categories.label: Knowledge
categories.description: RAG knowledge base retrieval and management

categories.label: Notifications
categories.description: Send messages to external channels (Slack, Teams, email, etc.)

categories.label: NLP
categories.description: Intent classification, entity extraction, and text analysis

node_types.label: Webhook Trigger
node_types.description: Start workflow on HTTP POST

method.description: HTTP method accepted by this trigger endpoint

path.description: URL path for this webhook (e.g. /my-webhook). Must start with /

node_types.label: Schedule Trigger
node_types.description: Start workflow on a cron schedule

cron.description: Cron expression in UTC (e.g. '0 * * * *' = every hour, '0 9 * * 1-5' = weekdays at 9am)

node_types.label: LLM Agent
node_types.description: AI reasoning node with prompt and model selection

provider.description: LLM provider to use for this agent

model.description: Model variant — balance speed vs. capability

systemPrompt.description: Jinja2 template for the system prompt. Use {{ trigger.field }} for trigger data or {{ node_2.response }} for a previous node's output

temperature.description: Sampling temperature — 0 = deterministic, 1 = balanced, 2 = very creative

maxTokens.description: Maximum number of output tokens for this LLM call

node_types.label: ReAct Agent
node_types.description: Iterative tool-calling agent loop

provider.description: LLM provider powering the ReAct reasoning loop

model.description: Model variant used for each reasoning step in the ReAct loop

systemPrompt.description: Jinja2 template for the system prompt. Use {{ trigger.field }} or {{ node_2.response }}

maxIterations.description: Maximum number of Reason→Act cycles before the agent stops (prevents runaway loops)

tools.description: MCP tools available to this agent. Leave empty to auto-discover all tools at runtime

node_types.label: MCP Tool
node_types.description: Execute an MCP server tool

toolName.description: Name of the MCP tool to call — select from the list or type manually

parameters.description: JSON object of fixed parameters to pass to the tool. Runtime node outputs can override these

node_types.label: HTTP Request
node_types.description: Make an external HTTP call

method.description: HTTP method to use for the request

body.description: Request body text — used for POST, PUT, and PATCH requests

node_types.label: Human Approval
node_types.description: Pause and wait for human approval

approvalMessage.description: Message shown to the approver in the approval UI

timeout.description: Seconds to wait for approval before the execution times out (3600 = 1 hour)

node_types.label: Bridge User Reply
node_types.description: Choose the exact text a sync caller receives when using the orchestrator bridge (sets orchestrator_user_reply)

messageExpression.description: Optional safe_eval expression for the reply (e.g. node_7.response). If set, takes precedence over responseNodeId

responseNodeId.description: Node id whose response or output field becomes the chat reply (same idea as Save Conversation State)

node_types.label: Condition
node_types.description: Branch based on a condition expression

condition.description: safe_eval expression evaluated as true/false (e.g. node_2.intent == "diagnose" or node_3.score > 0.8)

trueLabel.description: Label for the edge followed when the condition is true

falseLabel.description: Label for the edge followed when the condition is false

node_types.label: Merge
node_types.description: Merge multiple branches

strategy.description: waitAll: hold until every incoming branch completes; waitAny: continue as soon as any one branch completes

node_types.label: ForEach
node_types.description: Iterate over an array, executing downstream nodes once per element

arrayExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the array to iterate (e.g. node_2.items or trigger.records)

itemVariable.description: Variable name for the current element inside the loop (accessible as item.field in downstream nodes)

node_types.label: Loop
node_types.description: Repeat downstream body nodes while a condition expression is true. The loop runs up to maxIterations times (hard cap: 25). An empty continueExpression runs unconditionally for maxIterations.

continueExpression.description: safe_eval expression evaluated before each iteration. Loop continues while True (e.g. node_2.score < 0.9 or _loop_index < 3). Leave empty to run unconditionally for maxIterations.

maxIterations.description: Maximum number of loop iterations (backend hard cap: 25). Each body node re-executes once per iteration.

node_types.label: Sub-Workflow
node_types.description: Execute another saved workflow as a single step. Maps inputs from parent context to child trigger, and returns child outputs.

workflowId.description: ID of the workflow definition to execute as a sub-workflow

versionPolicy.description: Use the latest version or pin to a specific snapshot version

pinnedVersion.description: Version number to use when versionPolicy is pinned

inputMapping.description: Map of child trigger keys to parent context expressions evaluated via safe_eval (e.g. {"message": "node_2.response", "user_id": "trigger.user_id"})

outputNodeIds.description: Only return outputs from these child node IDs. Leave empty to return all child node outputs.

maxDepth.description: Maximum nesting depth for recursion protection

node_types.label: Load Conversation State
node_types.description: Fetch multi-turn chat history from persistent storage. Place this as the first node after the trigger to enable conversational memory.

sessionIdExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the session identifier (e.g. trigger.session_id)

node_types.label: Save Conversation State
node_types.description: Append the current user message and assistant response to persistent chat history. Place this as the last node in the graph.

sessionIdExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the session identifier

responseNodeId.description: Node ID whose 'response' field is saved as the assistant message (e.g. node_4)

userMessageExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the user's current message text

node_types.label: LLM Router
node_types.description: Classifies the user's intent into one of the configured intent labels. Outputs {intent: string} for downstream Condition nodes to route on.

provider.description: LLM provider used to classify the user intent

model.description: Model variant for the classification call

intents.description: Valid intent names the router can output (e.g. diagnose_server, casual_chat, escalate)

historyNodeId.description: Node ID of the Load Conversation State node to read history from (e.g. node_1)

userMessageExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the current user message text

node_types.label: Reflection
node_types.description: Calls an LLM with a summary of the workflow's execution history and returns a structured JSON assessment. Downstream Condition nodes can branch on any returned field (e.g. {"next_action": "escalate"}).

provider.description: LLM provider used for the reflection call

reflectionPrompt.description: Jinja2 system prompt template. Use {{ execution_summary }} to inject the auto-built history block, plus any other context variables.

outputKeys.description: Expected top-level keys in the LLM's JSON response (e.g. ["next_action", "confidence"]). A warning is logged for any missing keys but execution is not blocked.

maxHistoryNodes.description: Maximum number of recent node outputs to include in the execution summary (hard cap: 25)

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for the LLM call (0 = deterministic, 1 = creative)

maxTokens.description: Maximum tokens for the LLM response

node_types.label: A2A Agent Call
node_types.description: Delegate a task to an external A2A-compatible agent and wait for the result. The remote agent's response is injected into context for downstream nodes.

agentCardUrl.description: URL of the remote agent's /.well-known/agent.json discovery document

skillId.description: Skill ID to invoke on the remote agent. Leave blank to use the first available skill.

messageExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the message text sent to the remote agent (e.g. trigger.message or node_3.response)

apiKeySecret.description: Vault reference to the remote agent's A2A API key, e.g. {{ env.REMOTE_AGENT_KEY }}

timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the remote agent to complete. Raises an error if exceeded.

node_types.label: Code
node_types.description: Run sandboxed Python code for data transformation and custom logic

language.description: Programming language for the code snippet

code.description: Python code to execute in a sandboxed subprocess

node_types.label: Knowledge Retrieval
node_types.description: Search knowledge bases and return relevant document chunks for RAG. Outputs context_text for downstream LLM nodes.

knowledgeBaseIds.description: Knowledge bases to search. Select one or more from your tenant's KBs

queryExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the search query text (e.g. trigger.message or node_2.response)

topK.description: Maximum number of relevant chunks to return

scoreThreshold.description: Minimum similarity score (0-1). Chunks below this threshold are filtered out. 0 = no filtering

node_types.label: Notification
node_types.description: Send a formatted message to Slack, Teams, Discord, Telegram, WhatsApp, PagerDuty, email, or a generic webhook

destination.description: Webhook URL, bot token, or API endpoint. Supports: static value, {{ env.SECRET }} from vault, or {{ trigger.field }} / {{ node_N.field }} from runtime context.

messageTemplate.description: Jinja2 message template. Use {{ trigger.field }} or {{ node_2.response }} for dynamic content.

iconEmoji.description: Emoji icon for the Slack message

avatarUrl.description: Avatar image URL for the Discord bot

themeColor.description: Hex color for the Teams card accent

chatId.description: Telegram chat/group ID. Static, {{ env.TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID }}, or {{ trigger.chat_id }}

phoneNumber.description: Recipient phone in E.164 format. Static, {{ env.* }}, or {{ trigger.phone }}

phoneNumberId.description: WhatsApp Business phone number ID (from Meta dashboard)

templateName.description: Approved WhatsApp message template name. Required for business-initiated messages; leave empty for session replies.

to.description: Recipient email(s), comma-separated. Static, {{ env.* }}, or {{ trigger.email }}

subject.description: Email subject (Jinja2 template). E.g. Alert: {{ node_3.severity }} - {{ trigger.service }}

smtpPort.description: SMTP server port (only used when emailProvider is smtp). Common: 587 (STARTTLS), 465 (SSL)

smtpUser.description: SMTP login username (only used when emailProvider is smtp). Supports {{ env.SMTP_USER }}

smtpPass.description: SMTP login password (only used when emailProvider is smtp). Use {{ env.SMTP_PASS }} for vault storage

httpHeaders.description: Custom HTTP headers for generic webhook

node_types.label: Intent Classifier
node_types.description: Classify user intent using hybrid scoring (lexical + embedding + optional LLM). Production-grade upgrade over LLM Router with configurable intents, examples, and confidence thresholds.

utteranceExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the user utterance text (e.g. trigger.message or node_2.response)

intents.description: List of intents with name, description, examples, and priority

allowMultiIntent.description: Allow multiple intents to be returned when scores are close

mode.description: hybrid: heuristic first, LLM fallback when low confidence. llm_only: always use LLM. heuristic_only: lexical + embedding only, zero LLM cost.

provider.description: LLM provider for classification (used in hybrid and llm_only modes)

cacheEmbeddings.description: Precompute and cache intent embeddings at save time. Recommended for large intent lists (15+) or high-throughput workflows. When false, embeddings are computed on-the-fly at runtime.

confidenceThreshold.description: Minimum confidence for heuristic results. Below this threshold, hybrid mode falls back to LLM.

historyNodeId.description: Node ID of the Load Conversation State node to read history from (used in LLM classification prompt)

node_types.label: Entity Extractor
node_types.description: Extract structured entities from text using rule-based patterns (regex, enum, number, date, free_text) with optional LLM fallback for missing required entities.

sourceExpression.description: Expression that resolves to the source text for entity extraction

entities.description: List of entities to extract, each with name, type, pattern, and options

scopeFromNode.description: Node ID of an upstream Intent Classifier whose matched intents restrict which entities are extracted

intentEntityMapping.description: Map of intent name → array of entity names. Restricts extraction to entities relevant to the matched intent. Example: {"book_flight": ["destination", "date"]}

llmFallback.description: Use an LLM to extract required entities that rule-based extraction missed
